title: Mohd. Ahmed Khan v. Shah Bano Begum
legal_principles: Section 125 CrPC applies to all citizens regardless of religion
Personal law cannot override secular law
relevant_acts: Muslim Women (Protection of Rights on Divorce) Act 1986
summary: Shah Bano, a divorced Muslim woman, claimed maintenance under Section 125 CrPC. Husband claimed Muslim personal law (mehr was sufficient). Supreme Court held that Section 125 CrPC applies to all Indian citizens regardless of religion and upheld Shah Bano's right to maintenance. Court also commented on the desirability of a Uniform Civil Code.
key_holdings: Section 125 CrPC applies to all citizens regardless of religion
Divorced Muslim women are entitled to maintenance under CrPC
Payment of mehr does not absolve husband of maintenance obligation
Court expressed desirability of Uniform Civil Code under Article 44
key_quotes: Section 125 of the Code of Criminal Procedure is a secular provision which applies to all communities.
It is a matter of regret that Article 44 has remained a dead letter.
why_important: Generated enormous controversy and led to enactment of Muslim Women (Protection of Rights on Divorce) Act 1986 by Congress government, partly overturning the ruling. Sparked debate on uniform civil code.
relevance_to_legal_finance_system: Financial obligations in personal law (maintenance, alimony) have income tax and financial planning implications.
